message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit(). I need to own _termui_impl.py (Editor class) and termui.py (edit() function). Let me know if your feature touches these too.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both need to touch the same two files (Editor class ctor + edit() signature), so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing sequential turns instead: my change is small/additive (one new optional process_priority param, default None, no signature reordering). I'll go first, finish fast, and send DONE with the exact diff. You then layer your multi-file support on top of my changes. OK with you, or would you rather go first?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - sequential turns since files truly overlap. You go first with process_priority param, send DONE with your diff, then I'll layer multi-file support on top. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Confirmed. Implementing process_priority now, will send DONE shortly.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Changes: Editor.__init__ (_termui_impl.py) gained a process_priority: str|None=None param (validated against low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high, else ignored -> None), stored as self.process_priority. Added module-level _EDITOR_PRIORITY_LEVELS tuple and _EDITOR_POSIX_NICE_OFFSETS dict, plus a new Editor._get_priority_popen_kwargs() method that returns {'creationflags': ...} on Windows or {'preexec_fn': ...} on POSIX (calls os.nice(), swallowing OSError) - empty dict if unsupported/None. edit_file() now does  right before the existing  call and spreads  into it - the Popen call site's args/kwargs list is the only line-level change there, so should merge cleanly with unrelated additions. In termui.py, edit() gained a process_priority: str|None=None kwarg (last param, after filename) forwarded straight into  construction, plus a new :param: docstring line and versionadded note. No changes to edit()'s existing params, return logic, or the filename/text branching - safe to add your multi-file params alongside mine. Ran full tests/test_termui.py (62 passed) plus a manual click.edit() smoke test with a fake editor script and process_priority='low'/'high'/None - all fine. Diff is in patch.txt at repo root if you want to check exact hunks before layering. All yours now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Summary of changes - in _termui_impl.py: Editor.__init__ gained a process_priority param (str or None, default None), validated against a low/below_normal/normal/above_normal/high tuple else set to None. Added module-level constants _EDITOR_PRIORITY_LEVELS and _EDITOR_POSIX_NICE_OFFSETS, plus a new method Editor._get_priority_popen_kwargs() that returns Popen kwargs (creationflags on Windows, preexec_fn calling os.nice on POSIX, swallowing OSError), empty dict if unsupported or priority is None. In edit_file(), right before the existing subprocess.Popen call, I compute priority_kwargs via that method and spread it into the Popen call - only the Popen call site itself changed at that spot, so it should merge cleanly with unrelated edits. In termui.py: edit() gained a process_priority param (str or None, default None) as the last parameter after filename, forwarded directly into the Editor constructor call, plus a new docstring param line and versionadded note. No other params, return logic, or filename/text branching touched - safe for you to add multi-file params alongside mine. Ran tests/test_termui.py (62 passed, 21 skipped) plus a manual smoke test of click.edit() with a fake editor script and process_priority low/high/None - all good. Full diff is in patch.txt at repo root. All yours now to layer on top.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file click.edit() support. Renamed Editor.edit_file -> edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), joins quoted filenames for the editor subprocess command, edit() internally calls edit_files((name,)). In termui.py, edit()'s filename param now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None, added typing overloads, docstring update + versionchanged 8.2.0 note. Note: I don't see any process_priority changes in the working tree on my end (git diff/status was clean before I started, no process_priority references anywhere in src/) - just flagging in case there's a sync issue on your side, since our two features would both touch Editor.__init__/edit_file signatures and I want to avoid silently clobbering your work. My diff is in patch.txt at repo root - only touches the two agreed files.
